Title: GDP surprise fuels hawkish OPR bets, lifts bond yields
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Investors have turned more hawkish on Malaysia's interest rate outlook after the economy grew faster than expected in the second quarter, according to CIMB Securities Sdn Bhd.
The research house said government bond yields had risen as markets priced in a greater chance of a 25-basis-point hike in the overnight policy rate (OPR).
However, it expects Bank Negara Malaysia to leave the OPR unchanged at 2.75 per cent for the foreseeable future, saying the stronger growth has yet to translate into broad-based inflationary pressures.
"We maintain our call for the OPR to remain unchanged at 2.75 per cent through the first half of 2027," senior economist Lim Yee Ping and research head Michelle Chia said in a note.
Malaysian sovereign bonds cheapened by up to two basis points over the past week after advance estimates showed the economy expanded 5.8 per cent in the second quarter.
The figure beat CIMB Securities' forecast of 4.8 per cent and the market consensus of 5.2 per cent. Bank Negara will release the official data on Aug 14.
"The stellar growth strength remains concentrated in export-oriented sectors, particularly electrical and electronics manufacturing, and is unlikely to imply broad-based excess demand and inflationary pressures," it said.
Malaysia's headline and core inflation both eased to 1.9 per cent in June from 2.0 per cent in May, according to the Statistics Department.
CIMB Securities expects inflation to ease further next year as the recent fuel-driven uptick fades, keeping the real policy rate close to its estimate of the neutral level of around one per cent.
It also raised its 2026 gross domestic product growth forecast to 4.8 per cent from 4.3 per cent following the stronger-than-expected second-quarter performance.
Separately, Moody's affirmed Malaysia's A3 sovereign rating with a stable outlook, citing the country's strong medium-term growth prospects and revenue reforms that have broadened the tax base.
The research house also pointed to the government's indication that cash assistance under the Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah and Sumbangan Asas Rahmah programmes could be increased in the 2027 Budget, signalling a modestly expansionary fiscal stance.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PPR owners subletting units undermine housing scheme's purpose, says Johari
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The issue of People's Housing Project (PPR)
unit owners renting out their homes to third parties must be seriously addressed as it undermines the scheme's original objective of helping low-income groups.
Titiwangsa member of parliament Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani said Kuala Lumpur City Hall's (DBKL) decision to halt the sale of PPR
units was appropriate following cases of misuse by some owners.
He said there were instances of owners who obtained units at rental rates of about RM124 a month subletting them to others for up to
RM1,000 a month.
"Once ownership is transferred, some fail to pay maintenance fees and instead rent out the units to third parties, including foreigners. This goes against the purpose for which PPR housing was established," he said, as reported by Kosmo!.
Johari said this after attending a breakfast and meet-the-people session with residents of Perkasa PPR here yesterday.
The Investment, Trade and Industry minister, however, said the sale of PPR units could still be considered for genuinely eligible applicants, provided a more structured mechanism is put in place.
He also proposed that the government develop new housing
projects specifically for PPR tenants aspiring to own homes.
In addition, Johari suggested DBKL conduct block-by-block surveys among PPR residents to obtain majority consent before deciding on the sale of units.
"If sales are carried out based on majority agreement at the block level, the process will be more orderly and help prevent future issues," he said.
Johari added that all proposals would be formally submitted to DBKL to address issues related to the rental and ownership of PPR units.
He said about 70,000 applicants are currently on the waiting list for PPR units, while some existing owners continue to rent out their units instead of returning them to DBKL.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kelantan police station chief arrested over RM500 bribe from UNHCR card holder
Article:
KOTA BARU: A police station chief has been arrested on suspicion of accepting a RM500 bribe from a United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) card holder.
The 41-year-old suspect, who was serving as the officer in charge of a police station (OCS), was arrested by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) about 2.20pm yesterday.
It is learnt that he allegedly accepted the bribe in exchange for not taking action against the foreigner, a UNHCR card holder, who was operating a scrap metal business.
Kelantan MACC director Azmin Yusuff confirmed that the suspect was arrested under Section 17(a) of the MACC Act 2009.
The MACC also obtained a seven-day remand order against the suspect after he was produced before the Magistrate's Court here this morning.
Magistrate Amirul Asyraf Abdul Rashid granted the remand order until July 26 under Section 117 of the Criminal Procedure Code.
Earlier, the suspect, clad in the MACC's orange lock-up attire, arrived at the court about 9.05am, escorted by two MACC officers.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Four remaining MV Berkat Tuah crew members return home safely
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The four remaining Malaysian crew members of the MV Berkat Tuah, who were stranded at Hamriyah Port in Sharjah, United Arab Emirates, returned home safely today.
In a statement, the Foreign Ministry said the crew members arrived at Kuala Lumpur International Airport at 9am.
They were allowed to disembark from the offshore support vessel on July 18 following continued engagement with the Sharjah authorities by the Malaysian Consulate-General in Dubai and the Marine Department.
Four other Malaysian crew members from the same vessel had earlier returned to Malaysia on April 10.
The ministry said the consulate-general had conducted several visits to the stranded crew members to ensure their welfare and safety before their return.
It also worked closely with the Sharjah authorities, the Malaysian Marine Department, and other relevant parties to facilitate their repatriation.
"The Malaysian government expresses its gratitude and appreciation to the UAE government for its assistance and cooperation in facilitating the safe return of the MV Berkat Tuah crew members," it said.
The ministry said
Malaysian diplomatic missions abroad
remained committed to safeguarding the welfare of Malaysians overseas.
The vessel had been stranded at Hamriyah Port amid the conflict in the Middle East, which erupted following a wave of United States and Israeli attacks on Iran on Feb 28.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China leaves benchmark lending rates unchanged for 14th consecutive month in July
Article:
SHANGHAI: China kept benchmark lending rates unchanged for a 14th consecutive month on Monday, in line with market expectations.
The unchanged loan prime rates (LPRs) suggest that policymakers remain patient, keeping interest rates on hold despite softer-than-expected second-quarter economic data that underscored the uneven growth in the world's second-largest economy.
The one-year LPR was kept at 3.00 per cent, while the five-year LPR was unchanged at 3.50 per cent.
In a Reuters survey of 23 market participants conducted last week, all participants predicted no change to either of the two rates.
China's economy expanded at its slowest pace in more than three years in the second quarter, missing forecasts, with weak household consumption clouding strong manufacturing and exports and intensifying concerns over the long-term sustainability of its unbalanced growth model.
China's economy is facing a structural mismatch between strong supply and weak demand, the People's Bank of China (PBOC) said earlier this month, pledging to maintain an appropriately loose monetary policy and ramp up financial support to revive domestic consumption.
Attention will be shifted to the upcoming Politburo meeting, where policymakers are expected to set the economic policy agenda for the second half of the year.
"All eyes are now on the end-July economy-focused Politburo meeting," said Kelvin Lam, senior China+ economist at Pantheon Macroeconomics.
"We will be looking for signs that policymakers recognise the importance of stabilising household balance sheets, potentially preparing a more comprehensive plan to stabilise the property sector and break the negative feedback loop between falling asset prices and weakening consumer confidence."
"On the monetary side, low but positive inflation shouldn't impede further People's Bank of China easing if it is deemed necessary," said Lynn Song, chief economist for Greater China at ING.
"Policymakers have made efforts to maintain ample liquidity, and we expect there is a solid chance we will see a rate cut within the quarter."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China securities regulator to hold market stability meeting, state media reports
Article:
SHANGHAI/BEIJING: China's securities watchdog will meet market participants on Monday to discuss market stability, official media said, as state-backed investors bought shares in an effort to stem a rapid slide in prices.
The move follows a rout that wiped out 10 trillion yuan (US$1.48 trillion) worth of China market capitalisation over the past two weeks. Two state-owned firms said over the weekend they had deployed roughly 60 billion yuan to buy stocks.
The China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC) has invited market participants to a meeting on Monday to listen to their proposals on how to promote stable and healthy development of the market, the official Securities Times reported.
The CSRC will hold seminars with representatives from brokerages, fund management firms and listed companies in the coming days to collect their opinions for policy-making to promote market stability, the official China Securities Journal said.
China's stock market tumbled more than 5 per cent last week as chipmaker CXMT's US$8.6 billion IPO stirred liquidity concerns, while a global sell-off in chip stocks and renewed conflicts in the Middle East also dampened risk appetite.
Selling was especially savage in tech stocks, with Shanghai's STAR Market plunging roughly 25 per cent from its July 1 peak.
China Reform Holdings Corp, owned by China's central government, said late on Sunday that it had spent 50 billion yuan buying stocks to stabilise the market and will continue to increase equity holdings.
The company said it "is unwaveringly confident in the outlook for China's capital markets, and will resolutely support tech innovation and high-quality growth of state-owned companies."
China Chengtong Holdings Group said in a separate statement that it had increased stock holdings worth nearly 10 billion yuan, vowing to "maintain capital markets stability with full force."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Foreign funds extend buying streak, net inflows jump eightfold to RM612.3mil
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Foreign institutions extended two consecutive weeks of net buying, recording RM612.3 million in net inflows, around eight times higher than the previous week's inflows of RM75.3 million, according to MBSB Investment Bank Bhd (MBSB IB).
According to its fund flow report for the week ended July 17, 2026, foreign institutions were net buyers on four out of five trading days during the week, with the highest inflows recorded on Thursday (RM238.7 million), followed by Tuesday (RM174.1 million), Wednesday (RM140.8 million) and Friday (RM92.1 million).
"The only outflows were seen on Monday (-RM33.4 million)," it said.
MBSB IB said the top three sectors that recorded net inflows from foreign institutions were financial services (RM754.1 million), utilities (RM194.9 million), and plantation (RM66.9 million).
The largest net outflows were recorded in industrial products and services (-RM256.0 million), technology (-RM98.5 million), and consumer products and services (-RM64.3 million).
Meanwhile, the local institutions extended their second consecutive week of net selling, recording net outflows of -RM305.9 million.
Retailers returned to net selling after the previous week of buying, recording net outflows of RM306.4 million.
The average daily trading volume saw a broad-based rise, with retailers up by 34.3 per cent, local institutions by 25.8 per cent, and foreign institutions by 40.8 per cent, MBSB IB said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Remains found in France belong to woman missing since 2020: Prosecutor
Article:
PARIS: Human remains found in France last week belong to a woman missing since 2020, a prosecutor said on Sunday, after her husband confessed to killing her and hiding her body.
In a case that has gripped France since the height of the Covid-19 pandemic, Cedric Jubillar, 38, was sentenced to 30 years in prison in October for murdering Delphine Jubillar, 33.
Her body had never been found and Jubillar had always maintained his innocence, including during the trial.
But in a dramatic about-turn earlier this month ahead of his appeal trial, Jubillar confessed for the first time to his involvement and pledged to lead investigators to where the body was concealed, according to his legal team.
This led to intensive searches last week over two days on agricultural land in Mailhoc in the Tarn department in southern France some 10 kilometres from the couple's former house.
Human remains were found there and they have now been confirmed to belong to Delphine Jubillar after DNA analysis at a laboratory outside Paris, said Toulouse prosecutor Nicolas Jacquet.
The victim's body was likely scattered accidentally during agricultural work.
Jubillar was convicted on the basis of a string of corroborating evidence, despite the lack of a body or any DNA evidence linking him to the crime.
The trial heard Delphine had asked for a divorce and had been seeking to start a new life with another man.
The couple had two children whose lawyers hope can now find some closure after their father's revelations.
Commentators have suggested Cedric Jubillar is looking to change his strategy ahead of his appeal trial by admitting the killing of Delphine but portraying it as an accident during a domestic dispute, in the hope of being given a lesser sentence.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: World Cup final crowd leaves Dataran Merdeka strewn with rubbish
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) has expressed disappointment over the amount of rubbish left behind at Dataran Merdeka after thousands gathered to watch the FIFA World Cup final between Spain and Argentina early yesterday.
In a Facebook post, DBKL said that while the gathering reflected the public's passion for football, the historic square was left strewn with litter following the celebrations.
"Early this morning, thousands gathered to cheer, celebrate football and share in the excitement. However, when the cheers ended, this historic field was left covered with rubbish.
"DBKL will clean it. Our personnel will collect what has been left behind, but the cleanliness of Kuala Lumpur should not always depend on others cleaning up after us," it said.
DBKL reminded the public that Dataran Merdeka was a symbol of the nation's pride and history, and the way people left the site reflected how much they valued it.
It said people were free to celebrate and enjoy public events, but that freedom should not come at the expense of keeping shared public spaces clean.
"A great city is not judged only by the crowds it attracts, but by the condition in which it is left after they have gone.
"Kuala Lumpur is our home. Let us care for it as we would our own home," it said.
The post came after images of rubbish strewn across Dataran Merdeka circulated on social media, drawing criticism from netizens and renewing calls for greater civic responsibility at public gatherings.
Among those commenting on the post, social media user  @Ryhan Mvsc IV, said: "The attitude of our people needs to change."
User @Syda Khld also expressed disappointment, writing: "It's sad to see."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: We'll lose in Negri if Malay vote is split, KJ says on BN-PN tie-up
Article:
SHAH ALAM: Cooperation between Barisan Nasional and Perikatan Nasional in the Negri Sembilan state election could lead to a landslide victory for both coalitions if Malay votes are not split, says former Umno Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin.
Acknowledging dissatisfaction among several division leaders in Negri Sembilan over seat allocations to Pas and Wawasan, Khairy warned that a split in the Malay vote would inevitably result in defeat.
Citing his defeat in the Sungai Buloh parliamentary seat during the last general election, Khairy noted that Pakatan Harapan won after securing around 50,000 votes against his 48,000.
"Even though Pas did not campaign aggressively then, they still won 29,000 votes," he said after a Subang Umno Youth divisional meeting last night.
"This shows that as long as a Pas candidate is contesting, the Malay vote will be split."
While acknowledging the anxiety among Umno members over the BN-PN partnership in Negri Sembilan, he said the state's political dynamics differed from those in Johor.
He said that BN in Johor was the incumbent holding the menteri besar post, backed by a strong four-year track record of rapid development and solid ground support.
In contrast, Khairy said BN in Negri Sembilan was merely riding along in a PH-led administration, where PH held the menteri besar post, controlled state and federal machinery, and operated in a state with a higher non-Malay voter population.
This, he said, required flexible leadership and smart strategic thinking.
Khairy also claimed that friction remained between BN and PH despite four years of co-existing in the unity government, adding that significant issues persisted within their cooperation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Op Tiris 4.0:  Ministry foils 12 attempts to siphon subsidised fuel worth over RM800,000
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry has foiled 12 attempts to siphon subsidised fuel and detained 27 people under Op Tiris 4.0 since the operation began in March.
Kuala Lumpur ministry chief enforcement officer Mohd Shahran Mohd Arshad said the 12 cases involved seizures worth more than RM800,000.
"Since the operation began until today, the ministry's Kuala Lumpur branch has recorded 12 cases under Op Tiris 4.0. Just last week, we uncovered another case involving diesel leakage at an illegal storage facility.
"The total value of seizures has exceeded RM800,000 for the 12 cases, while 27 local and foreign suspects have been detained," he said at a press conference at a petrol station along Jalan Tun Razak early today.
Earlier, Shahran led about 12 enforcement officers in a motorcycle patrol covering 20 petrol stations in Setapak, Segambut and along Jalan Tun Razak.
He said inspections conducted between midnight and 2am did not uncover any suspicious activity at the petrol stations checked.
"Under the Kita Gempur initiative and Op Tiris 4.0, we are carrying out proactive inspections, including undercover motorcycle patrols at petrol stations throughout Kuala Lumpur.
"This strategy is aimed at preventing the leakage and misappropriation of subsidised diesel. With the excitement surrounding the FIFA World Cup final, criminals may think they can take advantage of the distraction.
"We want to stress that KPDN Kuala Lumpur continues to carry out monitoring regardless of any major event to ensure there is no abuse of subsidised fuel," he said.
At the same time, he warned all parties, particularly petrol station operators, against colluding with syndicates involved in diverting subsidised fuel.
He added that diesel smuggling activities in Kuala Lumpur remain under control, with frequent inspections and patrols proving to be an effective deterrent.
"So far, we have detected several attempts to misappropriate subsidised diesel in the capital involving various modus operandi.
However, most of the storage facilities used to keep the stolen diesel are located outside Kuala Lumpur," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Israel PM's office slams NY mayor over Netanyahu arrest remarks
Article:
TEL AVIV: The office of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Sunday denounced New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani after he said he considering whether to arrest the Israeli leader during an upcoming UN summit.
"Mr Mamdani should focus on fixing the damage his policies have caused New York," Netanyahu's office said, in a post on its official X account.
"Like Karim Khan, Mamdani appears interested in diverting public attention from his follies and attacking the leader of the Jewish state and the only democracy in the Middle East."
In an interview published on Saturday, Mamdani said he was consulting New York City's legal team about the extent of the mayor's authority to direct police to detain a foreign leader, referring to Netanyahu.
"I believe that Prime Minister Netanyahu belongs in The Hague," Mamdani told The New York Times. "He's a war criminal who has been charged by the International Criminal Court."
"Whatever the law allows me to do in New York City, that's what we will do," he said.
Netanyahu's office hit back on Sunday against Mamdani and also the International Criminal Court.
"The ICC is a kangaroo court that has no jurisdiction over Americans or Israelis. Its bogus arrest warrant against Prime Minister Netanyahu was issued by a disgraced former ICC Prosecutor, Karim Khan," the office said.
It added that the warrant was issued just days before allegations of sexual misconduct were made against Khan, calling it "a clear attempt by Khan to divert public attention and seek protection from scrutiny."
Mamdani has in the past vowed to send the NYPD to enforce arrest warrants against leaders wanted by the International Criminal Court, including Netanyahu or Russian President Vladimir Putin.
The UN General Assembly, a major gathering of world leaders, takes place in September at UN headquarters in New York.
The Hague-based ICC said in 2024 that it had reasonable grounds to believe Netanyahu was responsible for alleged war crimes and crimes against humanity related to Israel's offensive in Gaza following the Oct 7, 2023 cross-border attack by Hamas.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: HLIB: PetDag fuel supply remains stable despite Middle East tensions
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Petronas Dagangan Bhd (PetDag) continues to maintain stable fuel supply and inventory levels despite ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, according to Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB).
HLIB said PetDag's integrated inventory management system, supported by automatic tank gauging technology and close coordination with Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas), has ensured uninterrupted fuel availability at petrol stations nationwide.
"Inventory is typically maintained at three to five days at petrol stations and three to 10 days at terminals, depending on product type and demand requirements.
"This is further supported by the government's assurance that Malaysia's petroleum and fuel supplies will remain sufficient through end-2026, backed by measures to safeguard energy security amid ongoing geopolitical uncertainties in the Middle East," it said in a note.
However, HLIB expects PetDag's retail segment performance to moderate in the second quarter (Q2) of financial year 2026 (FY26) following a sharp decline in crude oil prices.
Following the sharp increase in Brent crude prices during Q1, which lifted the retail segment's earnings before interest and tax margin to 5.1 per cent from 2.0 per cent in Q4 FY25, prices have since fallen by about 38 per cent, averaging US$73 per barrel in Q2 2026.
"We expect the retail segment to soften in Q2 as lower Brent prices normalise inventory gains, while commercial margins should improve as the jet fuel pricing lag eases, although weaker Jet A1 volumes may temper the recovery," it said.
HLIB maintained its "Buy" call on PetDag with an unchanged target price of RM21.34.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia opens higher on expected sector rotation
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia opened higher on Monday, supported by expectations that foreign investors would rotate out of technology stocks into traditional sectors such as banking, consumer and plantation counters.
At 9.05am, the benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) gained 1.14 points to 1,732.59 from last Friday's close of 1,731.45.
The benchmark index opened 1.20 points higher at 1,732.65.
Across the broader market, gainers outpaced losers 157 to 134, while 305 counters were unchanged, 2,139 were untraded and 12 suspended.
Turnover totalled 216.22 million shares worth RM58.0 million.
Rakuten Trade Sdn Bhd vice-president of equity research Thong Pak Leng said the local bourse bucked the regional trend after Wall Street closed broadly lower, weighed down by another round of sell-offs in semiconductor and artificial intelligence-related stocks, compounded by a surge in crude oil prices following escalating tensions between the United States and Iran.
"The sell-off in technology-related stocks spread across the Asian region, with South Korean and Taiwan markets declining by more than six per cent, while the FBM KLCI emerged as a contrarian performer, ending higher and surpassing the 1,730 threshold," he told Bernama.
The index is expected to trade within the 1,725-1,740 range today, he added.
Among heavyweight stocks, Malayan Banking Bhd (Maybank) fell four sen to RM11.16, Public Bank Bhd declined three sen to RM5.22, while CIMB Group Holdings Bhd and IHH Healthcare Bhd were unchanged at RM7.82 and RM8.50, respectively. Tenaga Nasional Bhd gained eight sen to RM14.38.
Among actively traded stocks, VS Industry Bhd shed half a sen to 22.5 sen, while Pegasus Heights Bhd, Ingenieur Gudang Bhd, PUC Bhd, Tanco Holdings Bhd and Aimax Bhd were unchanged at half a sen, four sen, four sen, 33.5 sen and one sen, respectively.
Among the top gainers, Panasonic Manufacturing Malaysia Bhd rose 29 sen to RM5.59, Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd added 28 sen to RM43.48, while Petronas Gas Bhd and Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd gained 18 sen each to RM17.62 and RM4.91, respectively. Sarawak Oil Palms Bhd increased 16 sen to RM5.19.
The top losers were led by APM Automotive Holdings Bhd, which fell seven sen to RM2.93. MN Holdings Bhd and Hong Leong Bank Bhd declined four sen each to RM2.80 and RM22.40, respectively, while RHB Bank Bhd and Cyberjaya Education Group Bhd slipped three sen each to RM8.50 and RM1.47, respectively.
Among the broader indices, the FBM Emas Index rose 18.4 points to 12,737.49, the FBMT 100 Index gained 16.09 points to 12,573.65, and the FBM Emas Shariah Index increased 43.59 points to 12,526.06.
The FBM Mid 70 Index added 56.10 points to 17,831.03, while the FBM ACE Index declined 5.10 points to 4,874.05.
By sector, the Financial Services Index fell 48.37 points to 20,486.98, while the Energy Index rose 9.46 points to 775.74. The Industrial Products and Services Index advanced 0.89 of a point to 187.62, while the Plantation Index gained 14.10 points to 9,497.87.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hungary's PM Magyar to ask chess champion Judit Polgar to become president
Article:
BUDAPEST: Prime Minister Peter Magyar said on Sunday that he would ask Judit Polgar, widely regarded as the greatest female chess player of all time, to take up the largely ceremonial role of Hungary's next President.
Magyar said in a Facebook post that Polgar, 49, could represent the nation's unity, and that on Monday he would meet Polgar and ask her if she would accept the nomination.
The move comes a day after President Tamas Sulyok signed a constitutional amendment ​passed by Magyar's ruling Tisza party that ends his term as head of state.
The legislation was part of Magyar's drive to dismantle former Prime Minister Viktor Orban's bastions of power, for which Magyar says he received a strong mandate from voters after ousting the right-wing leader in an April election landslide.
Parliament, where Magyar's party has a two-thirds majority that allows it to change any laws, will elect a new president who will be in office until a planned new constitution takes effect, or for a maximum of five years.
Judit Polgar and her two sisters - Zsuzsa (Susan) and Zsofia (Sofia) - along with Ildiko Madl won the 1988 Chess Olympiad for Hungary's female team, ending Soviet dominance.
Judit Polgar played in tournaments against men and became one of the top players in a male-dominated sport.
She is the most talented woman to have ever played the game and the only woman to have crossed the 2700-rating threshold that many consider the "Super GM" distinction.
In February, "Queen of Chess", a documentary about Polgar, was released on Netflix.
"I will meet her tomorrow, and will ask whether she is ready to serve our country in a new role until the new constitution is passed," Magyar wrote in a Facebook post.
"Judit Polgar's name has been synonymous with talent and perseverance for decades."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gold slips as oil prices advance, Fed rate hike voices grow
Article:
BENGALURU: Gold prices fell on Monday as an escalation in the Middle East conflict pushed Brent crude above US$90 a barrel, heightening inflation concerns as several US Federal Reserve policymakers signalled that interest rate hikes may be needed to curb price pressures.
FUNDAMENTALS
* Spot gold was down 0.4 per cent at US$4,000.55 per ounce as of 0058 GMT, after hitting a two week low on Friday and posting a 2.5 per cent weekly loss. US gold futures for August delivery eased 0.3 per cent to US$4,005.90.
* Asian share markets were hesitant on Monday ahead of a packed week of major technology earnings that will further test investor faith in the AI momentum.
* Oil prices jumped three per cent, with Brent surpassing US$90 a barrel.
* The US said it had completed a ninth consecutive night of attacks against Iran after earlier announcing that at least two US military personnel were killed in Jordan, while US allies in the region reported more Iranian attacks on Sunday.
* Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack added her voice to a growing chorus of policymakers arguing interest rates may need to rise to beat back persistent inflation, setting up a charged debate at the Fed's next meeting and the possibility of dissents at Chairman Kevin Warsh's second meeting at the helm.
* US consumer sentiment increased to a five month high in July, but the improvement is likely temporary as renewed conflict in the Middle East raises gasoline prices.
* High interest rates increase the opportunity cost of holding non yielding assets such as gold.
* COMEX gold speculators raised their net long position by 4,294 contracts to 119,147 in the week ended July 14, CFTC data showed.
* Gold discounts in India widened last week to a one month high as weak jewellery demand and hopes of lower prices kept buyers on the sidelines, while premiums in China were largely steady amid muted retail demand.
* Elsewhere, spot silver gained 0.9 per cent to US$56.42 per ounce, platinum was up 0.1 per cent at US$1,592.97 and palladium rose 0.3 per cent to US$1,251.74.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zizie Izette freed of abetting late husband Bung Moktar in RM2.8mil corruption case [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Datin Seri Zizie Izette A. Samad was acquitted by the Sessions Court today of three charges of abetting her late husband in obtaining RM2.8 million in bribes linked to Felcra Bhd's RM150 million unit trust investment.
Judge Rosli Ahmad ruled that the prosecution had failed to prove its case beyond a reasonable doubt, freeing the 48-year-old former actress-turned-businesswoman, who is the widow of the late Datuk Seri Bung Moktar Radin.
Zizie Izette broke down in tears after the verdict was delivered, while several members of her family watched from the public gallery.
She was seen cupping her face as a family member comforted her.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Economists see inflation manageable despite rising cost pressures, no OPR cut needed
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's inflation is expected to remain manageable this year despite mounting upside risks from higher global oil prices, electricity tariffs, weather-related food supply disruptions and volatile grain prices, economists said.
Public Investment Bank Bhd (PublicInvest) projected Malaysia's consumer price index to rise 2.1 per cent in 2026, while Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) maintained its forecast at 2.0 per cent.
Both research houses also see little need for Bank Negara Malaysia to ease monetary policy, with PublicInvest expecting the overnight policy rate to remain unchanged at 2.75 per cent through end-2026 as underlying inflationary pressures stay contained.
Malaysia's headline inflation eased to 1.9 per cent in June from 2.0 per cent in May, while core inflation also moderated to 1.9 per cent from 2.0 per cent.
Inflation averaged 1.9 per cent in the second quarter, compared with 1.6 per cent in the first quarter, suggesting price pressures have firmed but remain broadly under control.
PublicInvest said renewed tensions between the United States and Iran had pushed Brent crude oil prices above the upper end of its US$70 to US$85 per barrel forecast range for the second half of 2026, creating upside risks through higher energy, freight and insurance costs.
It said additional risks could come from electricity tariff base effects, potential adjustments under the Automatic Fuel Adjustment mechanism, weather-related food supply disruptions linked to El Niño, and volatility in global grain prices.
However, the bank said the government's Budi95 fuel subsidy programme should continue to shield households from the direct impact of higher fuel prices by maintaining subsidised RON95 petrol at RM1.99 per litre for eligible users.
Even so, it cautioned that higher oil prices could still filter through to the broader economy over time via higher logistics, food distribution and utility costs.
Despite these risks, PublicInvest said inflation remained largely cost-push rather than demand-driven, reducing the urgency for changes to monetary policy.
"We therefore still expect Bank Negara to remain patient. Resilient domestic activity warrants vigilance, but the bar for a hike remains high unless second-round effects broaden into core services inflation, wages, inflation expectations or ringgit-driven import costs," it said in a note.
Meanwhile, HLIB said the renewed blockade of the Strait of Hormuz had pushed Brent crude oil prices back above US$80 per barrel, reversing the temporary relief that saw prices fall below US$75 per barrel following the ceasefire announcement.
The bank said renewed geopolitical tensions could increase inflation risks in the second half of 2026 through higher energy, freight and insurance costs.
However, it expects Malaysia's inflation outlook to remain relatively insulated, supported by the government's Budi95 and Budi Diesel subsidy programmes as well as the country's diversified hydrocarbon supply sources.
HLIB added that while external headwinds could lead to broader price pass-through over time, existing fiscal support measures should help cushion the impact on consumers.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Asia shares hesitant as oil climbs, earnings loom
Article:
SYDNEY: Asian share markets were hesitant on Monday as the escalating conflict in the Gulf lifted oil prices and fanned fears of inflation, while a packed week of major technology earnings will further test investor faith in the AI trade.
Brent crude climbed three per cent to cross US$90 a barrel for the first time in more than a month as the US military started a ninth straight day of attacks against Iran, which in turn struck targets across the region.
The jump in fuel costs has revived worries about inflation even as US consumer price data surprised on the downside last week, leading futures markets to price in 29 basis points of Federal Reserve rate hikes by year end.
"Our forecast is for a more gradual turn towards a Fed hike in 2027, but the balance of risks is shifting in the direction of an earlier hike than expected," said Bruce Kasman, chief economist at JPMorgan, noting a recent hawkish tilt in Fed policy rhetoric.
Futures imply a 60 per cent chance of a rate rise as early as September, pushing yields on 30 year Treasuries back above the psychological 5.0 per cent barrier. This is a level that tends to attract funds away from equities and towards fixed income, while lifting the valuation bar for future corporate earnings.
The shift has come just as investors question sky high valuations for chip and AI stocks, which has seen the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index shed 10 per cent last week to leave it 20 per cent below June's record high.
Markets took an added blow on Friday when Chinese AI firm Moonshot said it had a new open weight model, Kimi K3, that it says delivers performance approaching US giant Anthropic's frontier Fable model.
All of which raises the stakes for this week's rush of profit results, which include Alphabet, Intel and Tesla.
HIGH EXPECTATIONS FOR EARNINGS
BofA analyst Savita Subramanian remains upbeat on the earnings outlook, forecasting a 5 per cent beat versus consensus, or 28 per cent growth. Technology is expected to drive more than half of growth, with semiconductor companies expected to post growth of around 130 per cent year on year.
Such forecasts helped S&P 500 futures edge up 0.2 per cent in early trading, while Nasdaq futures firmed 0.4 per cent. In Europe, EUROSTOXX 50 futures and DAX futures both rose 0.2 per cent, while FTSE futures were flat.
Japan's Nikkei was closed for a holiday, having shed 6.4 per cent last week in a technology led rout. MSCI's broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan dipped 0.3 per cent.
South Korea's chip heavy market lost 0.6 per cent after diving almost 9 per cent last week in volatile trading as retail investors were squeezed out of leveraged positions.
The latest spike in oil will be a headache for the European Central Bank, which meets on Thursday and is widely expected to keep rates unchanged at 2.25 per cent following June's hike.
Markets have almost fully priced in a rate increase at its September meeting and rates of 2.75 per cent early next year.
The euro was flat at US$1.1433, having spent more than a week trading between US$1.1377 and US$1.1482. The dollar was steady at 162.41 yen, just below the recent 40 year peak of 162.84 as Japanese authorities flagged the threat of intervention should the yen weaken quickly.
In commodity markets, the rise in yields pressured non interest paying gold, which fell 0.6 per cent to US$3,993 an ounce.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: In China, people brave simulated typhoons to prepare for the real thing
Article:
HANGZHOU, (China):  Huang Zhen, 34, shut his cafe for a day last week to be buffeted by surging waters and powerful winds that knocked him off his feet — training for extreme weather events that are increasingly afflicting China.
Huang was one of a dozen people attending a session at the Lvxing Disaster Simulation Training Center in Hangzhou, eastern China.
For 1,599 yuan (RM960) per day, it offers a realistic experience of what it is like to be caught up in a typhoon or a flash flood, albeit with the advantage of wearing a wetsuit, a helmet and having a safety instructor to guide you.
The centre says it the first of its kind in the world to provide typhoon training, with its simulated experience generating wind speeds of up to 165 kph (103 mph) alongside torrential rain.
In a separate section, 900 metric tons of surging water are unleashed to mimic mountain flooding, while in another, participants must wade — sometimes thigh-deep, sometimes shoulder-deep — through 400 tonnes of water in a mock urban environment.
"Over the last two years, the weather has become increasingly unpredictable," Huang said.
"An opportunity like this ... gives us a better idea of what different levels of disasters might actually be like, and helps raise our awareness of disaster preparedness."
Climate change is increasingly exposing the world's second-largest economy to destructive weather events, scientists say.
This year is of ​particular concern due to the emergence of the El Niño weather pattern, which can drive up temperatures and fuel more intense typhoons.
China's National Climate Center has said it expects up ‌to six typhoons to form in the Northwest Pacific and South China Sea in July, more than the average of 3.8.
One of those was Typhoon Bavi, which brought severe floods and forced millions of Chinese to evacuate.
"Typhoon Bavi hit just a couple of days ago, making people realise that typhoon hazards are far more of a threat than we thought," said Wu Zijian, a 44-year-old freelance worker who travelled from nearby Shanghai to attend.
More than 160,000 people have attended sessions at the centre since it opened in May 2025.
Wang Kuanghan, the centre's chief instructor, said there are plans to open five more centres in China within the next three years.
These will include simulations of other natural disasters such as fires and earthquakes.
For those who can't make it there yet, Wang has a tip.
"If you ask me, the most important survival skill during a typhoon is simply not to go outside," he said.
"Stay indoors and keep yourself safe at home." — REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Brent oil tops US$90 as US, Iran intensify attacks in Middle East
Article:
SINGAPORE: Oil prices jumped three per cent on Monday, with Brent surpassing US$90 a barrel, as the United States and Iran expanded attacks in the Middle East that have curbed energy shipments in the Strait of Hormuz.
Brent crude futures climbed US$2.69, or 3.05 per cent, to US$90.79 by 2343 GMT, touching the highest level since June 11 and extending gains after rising 15.9 per cent last week, its biggest weekly gain since April.
US West Texas Intermediate crude was at US$84.68 a barrel, up US$2.19, or 2.65 per cent, the highest since June 12. Front month prices gained 15.5 per cent last week, the largest weekly increase since early March.
The Middle East conflict escalated over the weekend, with the US conducting a ninth straight night of attacks against Iran, while US allies Kuwait and Bahrain reported more Iranian strikes.
In recent days, both sides have taken aim at shipping traffic, with the US saying it is enforcing a naval blockade on Iranian ports, and Iran saying it targets vessels violating its rules on navigating the Strait of Hormuz, which usually handles one fifth of global oil trade.
A vessel was on fire northwest of Oman's Kumzar, the United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations agency said early on Monday.
"The coming days and weeks will provide a clearer picture of the sustainable level of oil exports from the region under renewed dual blockades," Barclays analyst Amarpreet Singh said in a note.
"As things stand, we think oil markets are still too complacent about the potential fallout for inventories, which, unlike at the beginning of the war, are at the tightest of the past five years."
Four vessels made the transit through the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday, down from eight the previous day, LSEG data showed. At least three oil products tankers and one Very Large Crude Carrier have entered the strait since Friday to load oil, the data showed.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ringgit opens higher against US dollar, major currencies
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit opened higher against the US dollar and major currencies on Monday, supported by stronger-than-expected advance estimates showing Malaysia's economy expanded faster in the second quarter of 2026 (Q2 2026).
At 8am, the local currency strengthened to 4.0920/0995 against the greenback from Friday's close of 4.0930/0990.
The country's economy expanded 5.8 per cent year-on-year in Q2 2026, according to advance estimates of gross domestic product (GDP) released by the Department of Statistics Malaysia on Friday, accelerating from 5.4 per cent in the first quarter.
Despite the stronger domestic economic data, Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said currency markets were likely to remain focused on developments in Middle East after casualties among United States (US) military personnel increased following exchanges between US and Iranian forces.
He said the US Dollar Index (DXY) remained well supported, rising 0.08 per cent from the previous close to 100.846 points.
"Against this backdrop, the ringgit is likely to trade on a softer note. On Friday, it closed 0.57 per cent lower against the greenback at RM4.0960.
"As such, the local currency could trade within a range of RM4.0900 and RM4.1165 today," he told Bernama.
The ringgit also traded firmer against a basket of major currencies.
It edged up against the British pound to 5.5017/5118 from 5.5018/5099 at Friday's close, strengthened versus the euro to 4.6759/6845 from 4.6824/6893, and appreciated against the Japanese yen to 2.5177/5225 from 2.5208/5246.
The local currency also traded higher against most regional peers.
It rose against the Singapore dollar to 3.1664/1727 from 3.1709/1758, strengthened versus the Indonesian rupiah to 228.3/228.8 from 228.4/228.8, and advanced against the Thai baht to 12.1504/1781 from 12.1743/1965.
The ringgit was unchanged against the Philippine peso at 6.64/6.66.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zizie Izette arrives at court to hear verdict on RM2.8 million corruption abetment case
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Datin Seri Zizie Izette Abdul Samad arrived at the Kuala Lumpur Court Complex today to hear the verdict on whether she will be acquitted or convicted on three charges of abetting in a corruption case involving RM2.8 million linked to a RM150 million investment in Public Mutual Berhad unit trusts.
Zizie Izette, 47, the wife of the late Kinabatangan Member of Parliament Datuk Seri Bung Moktar Radin,  arrived at the court at 8.30am.
Sessions Court Judge Rosli Ahmad is scheduled to deliver the verdict at 9am at the end of the defence case.
She is facing three charges of abetting her husband in receiving cash bribes totalling RM2.8 million.
The defence closed its case after Zizie Izette completed her testimony on March 5. Bung Moktar had testified on Sept 2, 2025, after the couple failed to overturn the Court of Appeal's decision ordering them to enter their defence.
On Nov 18, 2024, the Court of Appeal allowed the prosecution's appeal against the Sept 7, 2023, High Court's decision, acquitting the couple.
The High Court had overturned the Sessions Court's Sept 2, 2022 ruling, which had found that the prosecution had established a prima facie case and ordered Bung Moktar and Zizie Izette to enter their defence.
However, on Jan 16, the Sessions Court acquitted Bung Moktar of all three charges after the prosecution decided not to proceed with the case following the former politician's death on Dec 5, 2025.
Under the first charge, Zizie Izette is accused of abetting Bung Moktar, who was then the non-executive chairman of Felcra Berhad, in obtaining a RM2.2 million cash bribe from the investment agent of Public Mutual Berhad, Madhi Abdul Hamid.
The alleged bribe was an inducement for Bung Moktar to secure the approval of the then Finance Minister II for Felcra Berhad to invest RM150 million in Public Mutual Berhad unit trust products.
Under the second charge, she is accused of abetting Bung Moktar in obtaining RM262,500 from Madhi through a Public Islamic Treasures Growth Fund (PITGF) account registered in her name for the same purpose.
She also faces a third charge of abetting her husband in obtaining RM337,500 from another Public Mutual Berhad investment agent, Norhaili Ahmad Mokhtar, through a Public Ittikal Sequel Fund (PITSEQ) account registered in her name, also in connection with the same investment.
The offences were allegedly committed at the Public Bank Berhad Taman Melawati branch on June 12 and June 19, 2015.
The charges are framed under Section 28(1)(c) of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Act 2009, which provides for a maximum prison sentence of 20 years and a fine of five times the value of the gratification or RM10,000, whichever is higher, upon conviction. – BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Methane leak at Miri worksite confirmed, monitoring continues
Article:
MIRI: Methane gas detected leaking from a bore pile at a construction site along Jalan Perdana, Tudan yesterday has been confirmed as natural methane gas, prompting continued monitoring and a detailed technical assessment by multiple safety agencies.
Lutong Fire and Rescue Station chief Henry Jugah said firefighters received an emergency call at 7.45am and immediately dispatched a team to the site to conduct an
initial assessment
and gas readings.
Upon arrival, the team found no fire or explosion but began monitoring the surrounding area to ensure the construction site remained safe.
"A Petros team arrived at 9.40am to identify the gas leaking from the bore pile.
"Tests confirmed the gas was natural methane, with a reading of 100 per cent of the Lower Explosive Limit (LEL) directly at the source of the leak," he said in a statement.
Following the findings, the operation continued in the evening with the deployment of the Fire and Rescue Department's Hazardous Materials (Hazmat) unit from the Bintulu Fire and Rescue Station to conduct a more detailed technical assessment.
The Hazmat team arrived at 6.01pm and began taking measurements at 6.45pm.
The first reading recorded a 100 per cent LEL at the bore pile, while a reading of 32.5 per cent was detected within a one-metre radius of the leak.
A second assessment about 30 minutes later again recorded a 100 per cent LEL at the source, with a 32.5 per cent reading detected within a three-metre radius.
"The results show the gas remains concentrated around the leak source, and close monitoring must continue," Henry said.
As a precaution, the Fire and Rescue Department instructed the project developer to temporarily seal the bore pile until the Department of Minerals and Geoscience completes further investigations and assessments.
The operation also involved the police, Sarawak Energy Bhd and Petros to ensure the area remained safe.
The Hazmat operation concluded at 7.30pm after all safety measures had been completed, with no untoward incidents reported.
"No injuries were reported throughout the operation, and the surrounding area remained safe following continuous monitoring by all agencies involved," Henry said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Dollar firmer as US-Iran conflict intensifies, Brent hits US$90
Article:
SINGAPORE: The dollar made a modest advance against most of its global peers at the start of Asian trading on Monday as the conflict in the Middle East escalated, with oil prices rising and investor confidence remaining fragile after turbulence last week.
The US dollar climbed 0.1 per cent to 162.48 yen, the greenback's strongest level since July 9, as geopolitical tensions rose and investors sought safe haven assets.
The euro was down 0.1 per cent at US$1.1426, while the British pound was unchanged at US$1.3445. The Australian dollar slipped 0.1 per cent to US$0.6975, while the New Zealand dollar declined 0.2 per cent to US$0.5833.
"FX markets were relatively subdued, with the US dollar broadly stable, while the Australian dollar weakened against the greenback and most major currencies," Westpac analysts wrote in a research report.
"Market sentiment continued to deteriorate as concerns around semiconductor valuations weighed on risk appetite, while tensions in the Middle East escalated after Iran suspended its commitments under the interim peace deal."
Brent crude futures jumped 3.3 per cent to US$90.97 a barrel at the start of Asian trading after the US said on Sunday it had begun a ninth straight night of attacks against Iran after earlier announcing that at least two US military personnel were killed in Jordan.
Markets continue to anticipate no change to rates at the Federal Reserve's next meeting on July 29, with Fed funds futures pricing an implied 85.6 per cent probability of a hold, compared with a 61.5 per cent chance a month ago, according to the CME Group's FedWatch tool.
Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack on Friday added her voice to a growing chorus of policymakers arguing interest rates may need to rise to beat back persistent inflation, setting up a charged debate at the Fed's next meeting and the possibility of dissents at Chair Kevin Warsh's second meeting at the helm.
The US dollar index, which measures the greenback's strength against a basket of six currencies, was up 0.1 per cent at 100.84.
In cryptocurrencies, bitcoin was up 0.2 per cent at US$64,637.89, while ether was 0.2 per cent higher at US$1,869.72.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Samsung Biologics to launch US$1.8 billion all cash bid for PolyPeptide
Article:
BENGALURU: South Korea's Samsung Biologics will launch a 1.46 billion Swiss franc (US$1.81 billion) all cash bid for Swiss contract drugmaker PolyPeptide, the companies said in separate statements.
The offer of 44.31 Swiss francs per share represents a premium of about 6.1 per cent to PolyPeptide's last closing price of 41.75 Swiss francs on Friday, according to a Reuters calculation.
The tender offer is expected to launch by the end of August and close by year end, Samsung Biologics said.
The takeover would expand the South Korean firm's capabilities in peptide based therapeutics, including fast growing obesity and diabetes treatments such as GLP-1 drugs, the company said.
PolyPeptide said its board had unanimously recommended shareholders accept the offer.
Samsung Biologics said that after the deal was completed, it aimed to pursue a squeeze out of any remaining minority shares and would delist PolyPeptide from the SIX Swiss Exchange, making it a wholly owned unit of the company.
PolyPeptide said its largest individual shareholder, Draupnir Holding, which owns about 55.65 per cent of the company, supported the deal and would tender all of its shares into the offer.
The Swiss company said in April that Draupnir was conducting a review of strategic options regarding its majority stake in PolyPeptide.
Draupnir's parent entity is the Cryosphere Foundation, which has links to Swedish billionaire Frederik Paulsen, PolyPeptide has said previously.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: #NSTviral: Netizens react after gym-goer highlights matcha latte 'theft', confronts culprit
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A man's account of a stranger taking his drink at a gym has gone
viral on Threads
, drawing thousands of reactions after he shared CCTV footage and a play-by-play of the confrontation that followed.
The user, known on Threads as @myronwgm, shared that he had purchased the drink and left it aside to chat with a friend.
When he returned, the drink had vanished.
Determined to find out what happened, he reviewed the gym's CCTV footage.
The video revealed another man taking the drink and bringing it over to a treadmill.
Armed with this evidence, @myronwgm approached the man to ask if he had seen the missing matcha.
According to the post, the man initially denied seeing the drink, until he was confronted with the CCTV footage.
The dispute then moved to repayment.
The user claimed the man was reluctant to pay the full price of the drink, initially offering only RM10.
It was only after a threat to lodge a police report that the individual agreed to pay the full RM13.90.
Screenshots of the e-wallet transactions show the man first sent RM13, followed by a separate transfer of 90 sen after the owner insisted on the exact amount.
Adding a bizarre twist to the story, a gym cleaner later informed the owner that the man had reportedly taken the drink into the washroom to pour it into his own tumbler to hide the evidence.
The "matcha saga" has since garnered over 8,300 likes, with netizens in stitches over the pettiness of the situation and the effort taken to recover the funds.
"I need to know what brand of matcha this is if it's worth risking everything to steal it," user @fizachyu commented.
The story has become a viral reminder for gym-goers to keep a close eye on their belongings, including their refreshments.
View on Threads
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: E-invoicing curbs leakages, boosts national revenue
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The implementation of the e-invoicing system has enabled the Inland Revenue Board (IRB) to carry out more effective risk assessments, strengthening government efforts to curb leakages and boost national revenue.
Data analytics from e-invoices allow IRB to identify individuals or businesses with active financial transactions, including high-value purchases, who may have unreported income.
Treasury secretary-general Tan Sri Johan Mahmood Merican said the implementation of e-invoicing since August 2024 has seen more than 52,540 taxpayers voluntarily declare RM4.07 billion in additional taxable income, yielding RM1.009 billion in taxes due.
He said e-invoicing was not intended to burden traders or impose additional taxes, but to provide the government with transaction data for analysis and tax compliance.
"Data analysis from the system helps the IRB identify high-risk taxpayers for further review when transaction patterns or purchases do not match tax records," he added.
In line with the principle of transparency in the national taxation system, all taxpayers must issue an e-invoice for every sale of goods or provision of services exceeding RM10,000 starting Jan 1, 2026.
Buyers must provide their identification card numbers or Tax Identification Numbers (TINs) to sellers to ensure accurate e-invoice issuance.
"If checks reveal they are unregistered or have potentially unreported income, IRB will contact them for clarification," Johan said in an interview with Berita Harian.
Johan said this approach did not immediately lead to enforcement action. In the initial stages, IRB would issue letters to the individuals to allow them to review and update their income reporting if necessary.
"Based on e-invoicing implementation so far, data analysis shows there are individuals or businesses actively making purchases but who are unregistered or fail to report their income properly to the IRB," he said.
"Consequently, the IRB has contacted them to clarify if there are undisclosed sources of income. As a result, more than 52,540 taxpayers have voluntarily come forward to report approximately RM1 billion in additional taxes due.
"That is the result of the e-invoicing initiative," he said, adding that the government understood the system was new and that traders needed time to adapt.
Therefore, the government introduced a phased implementation and an additional relaxation period to allow traders to plan and execute e-invoicing smoothly.
To help taxpayers meet compliance requirements, Johan said the government had introduced the e-Invoicing Special Voluntary Disclosure Programme (PKPS).
"This allows traders to correct or complete their e-invoice records until Dec 31, 2027, without facing penalties," he said.
The government has also agreed to accelerate the full claim of capital allowances for the purchase of computers, software, and systems required for e-invoicing to a period of one year to help reduce compliance costs.
Johan said the use of e-invoicing data was also expected to strengthen government efforts to curb the shadow economy gradually, while increasing tax compliance without burdening law-abiding traders.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two teens killed, another in ICU after three-motorcycle crash
Article:
KUALA TERENGGANU: Two teenage motorcyclists were killed and another was severely injured in a three-motorcycle collision at Batu Rakit, Kuala Nerus on Sunday.
Kuala Terengganu police chief Assistant Commissioner Azli Mohd Noor said the deceased, aged 14 and 17, died at the scene. They were riding a Yamaha Ego and a SYM BX110 respectively.
The third rider, a 15-year-old who was on a Honda EX5, is currently fighting for his life in the intensive care unit of Sultanah Nur Zahirah Hospital.
Azli said the crash occurred when the 15-year-old, who was heading home towards Kampung Kubur Timah, was rammed from behind by the 17-year-old on the SYM BX110.
The impact forced the Honda EX5 into the oncoming lane, where it collided head-on with the Yamaha Ego Avantiz ridden by the 14-year-old.
The three motorcycles have been sent to Puspakom for vehicle inspection. The police are investigating the case under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act for causing death by reckless driving.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Airbus sees strong potential for A220 in Southeast Asia
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Airbus sees strong potential for its A220 aircraft in Southeast Asia as airlines in the region look to develop more long, thin routes and secondary-city connections.
Airbus Asia Pacific president Anand Stanley said the aircraft is suited for markets where geography, distance and route size made smaller narrow-body aircraft relevant.
"When you look at Indonesia as an archipelago, you look at Malaysia, which is connected or separated by the sea, and you look at the distances in Asia, the point-to-point long, thin routes are extremely valuable, and we believe that the A220 is the right aircraft for that market."
He told Business Times in an interview on the sidelines of the International Air Transport Association 82nd annual general meeting recently.
Stanley said the A220 programme had crossed 1,100 aircraft orders globally, while Airbus was also seeing strong order momentum from Asia Pacific.
He said Airbus had recorded 815 gross orders in the first five months of 2026, including 379 orders in May.
"A large number of these orders, over 50 per cent of the global Airbus orders of 815, and the majority of the 379 are all from the greater Asia Pacific region, including China," he said.
Stanley said Airbus is looking at demand for almost 20,000 new aircraft in Asia Pacific, with 16,000 of them single-aisle aircraft.
"We believe a lot of the single-aisle growth will also come from long, thin routes," he said.
He said Asean countries shared similar characteristics, including the need for inter-country, intra-country and intra-region connectivity.
"When you combine all this with the GDP growth and the demographics and the per capita increase in air travel that is going to come, I strongly believe that the A220 is a fantastic aircraft for full-service carriers, low-cost carriers, but also to be able to connect people in this region," he said.
AirAsia announced on May 6 that it had placed an order for 150 A220-300s at Airbus' facility in Mirabel, Canada.
The order is the largest single firm order placed for the A220 and made AirAsia a new customer for the aircraft.
AirAsia also has options for another 150 aircraft, giving the group flexibility to increase its commitment to 300 aircraft from the A220 family.
Deliveries are scheduled to begin in 2028.
The airline will also be the launch customer for the A220's new 160-seat cabin configuration.
AirAsia Group chief executive officer Bo Lingam had said in a media briefing recently that Philippines AirAsia would begin receiving the new A220 as part of the group's broader fleet renewal programme.
Stanley said AirAsia's order showed the aircraft also had a role in the low-cost carrier market.
"Now we see this news in the Southeast Asia, Asean region with a landmark order of 150 with AirAsia, which is a low-cost carrier."
"So now we can see that the A220, because it is a clean-sheet modern design aircraft, can be best-in-class not just with full-service carriers, but also with a low-cost carrier," he said.
Besides AirAsia, Stanley said the A220 is already being operated in Asia Pacific by Korean Air, Qantas and Air Niugini.
He said Korean Air was the first A220 operator in the region, followed by Qantas, which operates as a full-service carrier.
"The introduction and entry into service for Qantas has been extremely successful. Successful not just from a fleet reliability standpoint, but also from an outstanding customer satisfaction and NPS (net promoter score) score standpoint," he said.
Stanley said Air Niugini represented a different market for the aircraft, using the A220 on long, thin routes that required significant range.
"We have also seen the introduction of the A220 in Air Niugini, where they have huge, long, thin routes, very, very thin routes, but with a tremendous amount of distance that needs to be covered," he said.
Stanley said the A220 required its own type certificate but Airbus would provide the required transition training and support.
"The A220 does need its own type certificate. However, it is an Airbus aircraft, so we will provide whatever transition training is needed.
"We also provide the same Airbus team for support and services, the same Airbus team for the spare parts, the same Airbus team for the flight manuals, for the fleet support," he said.
On whether Malaysia could see an A220 simulator, Stanley said it was still early but there would be a need for supporting infrastructure as the fleet grows.
He said this would also involve wider support infrastructure in Malaysia and the region.
"There is a need for not just simulators but also the entire infrastructure and support infrastructure, not just in Malaysia, but in the entire Southeast Asia and Asean region."
"And there are plans to put that in place in line with the demand and growth that will come," he said.
The A220 was originally developed by Bombardier as the C Series before Airbus acquired a majority stake in the programme in 2018.
Airbus later introduced the aircraft as the A220 family, comprising the A220-100 and A220-300.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia hosts IAEA’s only Asia‑Pacific nuclear security equipment hub, bolstering global preparedness
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — Malaysia is increasingly playing a vital role in bolstering global nuclear security and emergency preparedness through practical international cooperation.
A cornerstone of these efforts is the country’s hosting of the International Atomic Energy Agency’s (IAEA) only equipment repository in the Asia-Pacific region, which supports the rapid deployment of specialised radiation detection equipment to strengthen nuclear safety and security during major public events across the region.
The repository at the Malaysia Nuclear Security Support Centre (NSSC) in Dengkil, Bangi, also serves as a regional hub for training and capacity-building, underscoring the country’s growing contribution to international nuclear security cooperation and risk management.
IAEA Division of Nuclear Security Director Elena Buglova said the repository forms part of the agency’s broader global nuclear security network by improving operational support and regional preparedness for nuclear security incidents.
“We have many practical arrangements around the world with different countries, but only in Malaysia do we have a practical arrangement with equipment covering the Asia Pacific region.
“We have made arrangement with them on where the equipment will be kept and for training to be conducted in Malaysia. When there is a need, they can be shipped to the country requiring assistance and returned to the hub in Malaysia,” she told Bernama during a seminar held at IAEA headquarters in Vienna recently.
Buglova said one of the repository’s most significant deployments came during the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar, where equipment from Malaysia was used for the first time to support nuclear security operations at stadiums against potential nuclear and radioactive threats.
“The equipment is stored there not only for training, but also for operational use during major public events and international assistance efforts.
“This demonstrates how the IAEA leverages the capabilities of member states and works with different partners to strengthen nuclear security,” Buglova said.
In September 2020, Malaysia, through the Department of Atomic Energy (Atom Malaysia, formerly the Atomic Energy Licensing Board), and the IAEA signed an agreement to establish a pool of radiation detection equipment available for loan.
According to the IAEA, the facility, established with financial contributions from Japan, supports nuclear security capacity-building and detection capabilities in the Asia-Pacific region, including for major public events.
The facility is also the first nuclear security equipment repository facilitated by the IAEA. Malaysia has been recognised as a NSSC by the IAEA since December 2012.
Under the agreement, the IAEA stations personal radiation detectors (PRDs), radionuclide identification devices (RIDs) and backpack radiation detectors (BRDs) at the Malaysia NSSC, which is operated by Atom Malaysia.
Meanwhile, Buglova said the IAEA, through its nuclear safety and security programme, has been assisting member states, upon request, in integrating nuclear security systems and measures into the overall security plans for organising major public events.
Since the Olympic Games in Greece in 2004, she said the agency has helped strengthen nuclear security measures and preparedness at almost 90 major public events in 49 countries, including sporting, political and religious gatherings.
Most recently, the IAEA assisted Mexico, one of the three co-hosts of the Fifa World Cup 2026, in strengthening nuclear security measures to protect tournament venues against malicious acts involving nuclear and other radioactive materials. — Bernama

Title: UniSZA pulls down 50 clinic banners after controversial ads spark backlash
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — Universiti Sultan Zainal Abidin (UniSZA) has removed 50 promotional banners for Klinik Perubatan UMC after the advertisements went viral and sparked controversy on social media.
In a statement issued by its Corporate Communications and Relations Management Centre, UniSZA said the banners were taken down after the issue was identified on July 13,
Sinar Harian
reported today.
The university said UniSZA Holdings Sdn Bhd (UHSB) was instructed to remove all the promotional materials within less than five hours of installation to prevent misunderstanding and discomfort among the public.
Of the 50 promotional buntings prepared, 25 contained the advertisement that sparked controversy, while the remaining materials featured other promotional content.
“Universiti takes this matter seriously and has instructed UHSB to conduct an internal investigation to identify the cause of the incident.
“This is to ensure all promotional materials issued in future comply with established procedures, guidelines and communication standards,” the statement said.
The operations of Klinik Perubatan UMC, including the management of promotional materials, fall under UniSZA Medical Services Sdn Bhd, a subsidiary of UHSB.
Following the incident, UniSZA also instructed UHSB to review the approval process for all promotional materials under its companies to ensure they are more thorough, professional and sensitive to public concerns.
UHSB, through UniSZA Medical Services Sdn Bhd, has since issued an official statement and apologised to the public over the confusion and concerns caused.
UniSZA said it would continue monitoring the improvement measures taken while valuing constructive public feedback to strengthen the university’s communication governance.

Title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain warnings issued for several states including Selangor, Penang and Sarawak
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning covering parts of 10 states and Putrajaya, with heavy rain and strong winds expected until early afternoon today.
On its Facebook page, MetMalaysia said the warning, issued at 8.50am, involved areas in Kedah, Penang, Kelantan, Terengganu, Pahang, Selangor, Putrajaya, Negeri Sembilan and Sarawak.
The affected areas include Kuala Muda in Kedah; Penang; Machang, Pasir Puteh, Kuala Krai and Gua Musang in Kelantan; as well as Besut, Setiu, Kuala Nerus and Hulu Terengganu in Terengganu.
The warning also covers Jerantut and Kuantan in Pahang, Klang, Kuala Langat and Sepang in Selangor, Seremban, Port Dickson and Rembau in Negeri Sembilan, and Matu, Dalat and Mukah in Sarawak’s Mukah division.
MetMalaysia said thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds were expected in the affected areas until 1pm today.
It said a thunderstorm warning is issued when there are signs of thunderstorms producing rainfall exceeding 20mm per hour and expected to continue for more than an hour.
The department added that such warnings are short-term alerts, valid for no more than six hours from the time of issuance.

Title: DBKL seizes equipment, issues 19 compounds against unlicensed photographers in KLCC area
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) issued 19 compound notices and seized equipment from individuals allegedly operating as unlicensed photographers during an enforcement operation around the KLCC area on Saturday night.
In a post on its Facebook page, DBKL said the integrated operation, dubbed Op Lens: Ulat Foto, was carried out with the cooperation of the National Registration Department (JPN) and Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) at Saloma Link, Jalan Ampang and Jalan P Ramlee at about 9pm on July 18.
The operation targeted unauthorised photography services, commonly referred to as “ulat foto”, being offered at public spaces, DBKL said in a statement shared on its Facebook page.
The local authority said it issued 19 compound notices to Malaysians found operating photography services without permission and seized 28 items of equipment, including mobile phones, with seven of the seizures involving foreign nationals.
The enforcement action was carried out under the Licensing of Hawkers By-Laws (Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur) 2016.
Meanwhile, JPN conducted checks on 28 individuals to verify their identity documents and passports, including one case involving the seizure of documents related to a permanent resident.
DBKL said it would continue conducting monitoring and enforcement operations to ensure public areas remain safe from unauthorised photography activities, which could pose security risks, cause inconvenience to visitors and obstruct traffic flow.
The seized equipment, including mobile phones, lighting equipment and gimbals, was transferred to the DBKL seizure store for documentation and further action.
Members of the public can submit complaints or feedback through DBKL’s official channels.

Title: ‘Celebrate, don’t trash’: DBKL slams World Cup final fans over Dataran Merdeka litter
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) has reminded the public to take responsibility for keeping shared spaces clean after Dataran Merdeka was left strewn with rubbish following a World Cup 2026 final viewing event.
In a Facebook post today, DBKL said it was saddened by the condition of the historic site after thousands of football fans gathered there early Monday morning to watch the final between Spain and Argentina.
“Early this morning, thousands gathered to cheer, celebrate football and share the excitement. However, when the cheers ended, this historic field was left covered with rubbish,” it said.
DBKL said its cleaning crews would be deployed to clear the waste left behind, but stressed that maintaining Kuala Lumpur’s cleanliness should not always depend on others cleaning up after public events.
“Dataran Merdeka is a symbol of the nation’s dignity and history. The way we leave it reflects how we value it,” it said.
The city council said people could celebrate enthusiastically but should not treat public spaces as disposable.
“We can celebrate in high spirits. We can cheer as much as we want, but the freedom to have fun never means the freedom to dirty shared spaces,” it added.
DBKL said a great city was not only judged by how it looked when people gathered, but also by the condition left behind after they left.
“Kuala Lumpur is our home. Take care of it like we take care of our own homes,” it said.
Thousands of fans had gathered at Dataran Merdeka to watch the World Cup final on a giant screen, with Spain defeating Argentina in the tournament’s title match.

Title: Court acquits Zizie Izette of three charges linked to RM2.8m bribery case
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — Actress Datin Seri Zizie Izette Abdul Samad was acquitted today of three charges of abetting in a corruption case involving RM2.8 million linked to a RM150 million investment in Public Mutual Berhad unit trusts.
Sessions Court judge Rosli Ahmad ruled that the defence had successfully raised reasonable doubt against all three charges faced by the 48-year-old widow of former Kinabatangan MP Datuk Seri Bung Moktar Radin, Kosmo! Online reported.
The prosecution was represented by Deputy Public Prosecutors Law Chin How and Mohd Fadhly Mohd Zamry, while Zizie Izette was represented by lawyers K. Kumaraendran, M. Athimulan and Ridha Abdah Subri.
Zizie Izette was accused of abetting Bung Moktar, who was then Felcra Berhad’s non-executive chairman, in receiving RM2.2 million in cash bribes from Public Mutual Berhad investment agent Madhi Abdul Hamid.
The alleged gratification was said to be an inducement for Bung Moktar to obtain approval from the then Finance Minister II for Felcra Berhad to invest RM150 million in Public Mutual Berhad unit trust products.
She also faced a second charge of abetting Bung Moktar in receiving RM262,500 from Madhi through a Public Islamic Treasures Growth Fund (PITGF) account registered under her name for the same purpose.
The third charge involved an alleged RM337,500 received from another Public Mutual Berhad investment agent, Norhaili Ahmad Mokhtar, through a Public Ittikal Sequel Fund (PITSEQ) account registered under her name.
The offences were allegedly committed at Public Bank Berhad’s Taman Melawati branch here on June 12 and June 19, 2015, under Section 28(1)(c) of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission Act 2009.
If convicted, she faced a maximum jail term of 20 years and a fine of five times the value of the gratification or RM10,000, whichever was higher.
On January 16, the Sessions Court acquitted Bung Moktar of the same three corruption charges after the prosecution decided not to proceed with the case following his death on December 5, 2025.

Title: Khairy: Malay vote unity could help BN, Perikatan reclaim Selangor
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — Former Umno Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin said Selangor could be won back if Malay votes were no longer split, pointing to possible cooperation between Barisan Nasional (BN) and Perikatan Nasional (PN) as a potential formula.
He said Selangor’s voter profile and demographics were similar to Negeri Sembilan, allowing the outcome of the upcoming state election there to serve as a reference for future political strategies, Kosmo! Online reported today.
